Variables.Name: TestOrder
Variables.Description: Number of test as defined in data asset

Variables.Name: TestPurpose
Variables.Description: The purpose of this test.  Should it be used for filtering possible results, scoring them, or both?

Variables.Name: TestComment
Variables.Description:
Optional comment or explanation about what this test is for.  Useful when the purpose of tests may not be clear,
	   especially when there are multiple tests of the same type.

Variables.Name: MultipleContextFilterOp
Variables.Description: Determines filtering operator when context returns multiple items

Variables.Name: MultipleContextScoreOp
Variables.Description: Determines scoring operator when context returns multiple items

Variables.Name: FilterType
Variables.Description: Does this test filter out results that are below a lower limit, above an upper limit, or both?  Or does it just look for a matching value?

Variables.Name: BoolValue
Variables.Description: Desired boolean value of the test for scoring to occur or filtering test to pass.

Variables.Name: FloatValueMin
Variables.Description: Minimum limit (inclusive) of valid values for the raw test value. Lower values will be discarded as invalid.

Variables.Name: FloatValueMax
Variables.Description: Maximum limit (inclusive) of valid values for the raw test value. Higher values will be discarded as invalid.

Variables.Name: ScoringEquation
Variables.Description: The shape of the curve equation to apply to the normalized score before multiplying by factor.

Variables.Name: ClampMinType
Variables.Description:
How should the lower bound for normalization of the raw test value before applying the scoring formula be determined?
	    Should it use the lowest value found (tested), the lower threshold for filtering, or a separate specified normalization minimum?

Variables.Name: ClampMaxType
Variables.Description:
How should the upper bound for normalization of the raw test value before applying the scoring formula be determined?
	    Should it use the highest value found (tested), the upper threshold for filtering, or a separate specified normalization maximum?

Variables.Name: NormalizationType
Variables.Description: Specifies how to determine value span used to normalize scores

Variables.Name: ScoreClampMin
Variables.Description: Minimum value to use to normalize the raw test value before applying scoring formula.

Variables.Name: ScoreClampMax
Variables.Description: Maximum value to use to normalize the raw test value before applying scoring formula.

Variables.Name: ScoringFactor
Variables.Description: The weight (factor) by which to multiply the normalized score after the scoring equation is applied.

Variables.Name: ReferenceValue
Variables.Description:
When specified gets used to normalize test's results in such a way that the closer a value is to ReferenceValue
	 	the higher normalized result it will produce. Value farthest from ReferenceValue will be normalized
	 	to 0, and all the other values in between will get normalized linearly with the distance to ReferenceValue.

Variables.Name: bDefineReferenceValue
Variables.Description: When set to true enables usage of ReferenceValue. It's false by default

Variables.Name: bWorkOnFloatValues
Variables.Description:
When set, test operates on float values (e.g. distance, with AtLeast, UpTo conditions),
	   otherwise it will accept bool values (e.g. visibility, with Equals condition)
